framework_name: SOC 2 Type II - Trust Services Criteria
disclaimer: This report assists with SOC 2 Type II compliance readiness assessment against AICPA Trust Services Criteria (2017, revised 2022). It does not constitute a SOC 2 audit or replace examination by an AICPA-licensed CPA firm. This tool automates assessment of technical infrastructure controls; organizational, procedural, and governance controls require separate evaluation. 19 of 43 criteria included in this assessment require manual evaluation.

CC1.1.title: Demonstrates commitment to integrity and ethical values
CC1.1.description: The entity demonstrates a commitment to integrity and ethical values through code of conduct, ethics policies, and leadership behavior.
CC1.1.manual_steps: Review organizational code of conduct, ethics policies, whistleblower procedures, and evidence of management setting the tone at the top.
CC1.1.evidence_template: CC1.1 requires manual assessment. Review organizational code of conduct and ethics policies.

CC1.2.title: Board exercises oversight of internal control
CC1.2.description: The board of directors demonstrates independence from management and exercises oversight of the development and performance of internal control.
CC1.2.manual_steps: Review board meeting minutes, audit committee charter, and evidence of board independence and oversight of internal controls.
CC1.2.evidence_template: CC1.2 requires manual assessment. Review board governance and audit committee documentation.

CC1.3.title: Establishes structures, authorities, and responsibilities
CC1.3.description: Management establishes, with board oversight, structures, reporting lines, and appropriate authorities and responsibilities in the pursuit of objectives.
CC1.3.manual_steps: Review organizational chart, RACI matrices, and role definitions. Automated checks verify that IAM policies enforce separation of duties and that permissions are assigned through groups, not directly to users.
CC1.3.evidence_template: IAM policies were checked for administrative privilege restrictions and group-based permission assignment. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary} Manual review of organizational structures is also required.

CC1.4.title: Demonstrates commitment to competent individuals
CC1.4.description: The entity demonstrates a commitment to attract, develop, and retain competent individuals in alignment with objectives.
CC1.4.manual_steps: Review hiring procedures, training programs, background check policies, and security awareness training records.
CC1.4.evidence_template: CC1.4 requires manual assessment. Review HR policies, training records, and security awareness program.

CC1.5.title: Holds individuals accountable for internal control
CC1.5.description: The entity holds individuals accountable for their internal control responsibilities in the pursuit of objectives.
CC1.5.manual_steps: Review performance evaluation criteria, disciplinary procedures, and accountability mechanisms for control responsibilities.
CC1.5.evidence_template: CC1.5 requires manual assessment. Review accountability policies and performance evaluation criteria.

CC2.1.title: Obtains and uses relevant quality information
CC2.1.description: The entity obtains or generates and uses relevant, quality information to support the functioning of internal control.
CC2.1.manual_steps: Verify that CloudTrail and AWS Config provide the quality audit data needed for control decisions, and that security contact is registered for notifications. Manual review of information quality processes is also required.
CC2.1.evidence_template: Audit trail and notification infrastructure was verified: CloudTrail multi-region logging and log validation, AWS Config recorder presence and status, security contact registered. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

CC2.2.title: Internally communicates information for internal control
CC2.2.description: The entity internally communicates information, including objectives and responsibilities for internal control, necessary to support the functioning of internal control.
CC2.2.manual_steps: Review internal communication policies, security awareness training records, policy distribution processes, and evidence that control objectives are communicated to relevant personnel.
CC2.2.evidence_template: CC2.2 requires manual assessment. Review internal communication policies and security awareness training records.

CC2.3.title: Communicates with external parties
CC2.3.description: The entity communicates with external parties regarding matters affecting the functioning of internal control.
CC2.3.manual_steps: Review external communication procedures, incident notification SLAs, customer agreements, and regulatory reporting processes.
CC2.3.evidence_template: CC2.3 requires manual assessment. Review external communication procedures and incident notification policies.

CC3.1.title: Specifies objectives with sufficient clarity
CC3.1.description: The entity specifies objectives with sufficient clarity to enable the identification and assessment of risks relating to objectives.
CC3.1.manual_steps: Review risk management framework, documented security objectives, and risk appetite statements.
CC3.1.evidence_template: CC3.1 requires manual assessment. Review risk management framework and documented security objectives.

CC3.2.title: Identifies and analyzes risks
CC3.2.description: The entity identifies risks to the achievement of its objectives across the entity and analyzes risks as a basis for determining how the risks should be managed.
CC3.2.manual_steps: Automated checks verify that risk identification services (GuardDuty, Security Hub, IAM Access Analyzer) are enabled, findings are addressed, and a support role exists for incident management. Manual review of risk assessment processes and risk register is also required.
CC3.2.evidence_template: Automated risk identification services were verified: GuardDuty enabled and findings addressed, Security Hub enabled, IAM Access Analyzer active, support role exists. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

CC3.3.title: Considers potential for fraud
CC3.3.description: The entity considers the potential for fraud in assessing risks to the achievement of objectives.
CC3.3.manual_steps: Automated checks verify controls against privileged access abuse: no wildcard admin policies, no root access keys, OIDC trust policies with sub condition. Manual review of fraud risk assessment is also required.
CC3.3.evidence_template: Privileged access controls were verified: no wildcard admin policies, no root access keys, OIDC trust restrictions in place. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

CC3.4.title: Identifies and assesses significant changes
CC3.4.description: The entity identifies and assesses changes that could significantly impact the system of internal control.
CC3.4.manual_steps: Automated checks verify that change detection services are active: AWS Config records configuration changes, CloudWatch alarms detect CloudTrail and Config modifications. Manual review of change impact assessment processes is also required.
CC3.4.evidence_template: Change detection infrastructure was verified: AWS Config recorder active, CloudWatch alarms for CloudTrail and Config configuration changes. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

CC4.1.title: Selects and performs ongoing evaluations
CC4.1.description: The entity selects, develops, and performs ongoing and/or separate evaluations to ascertain whether the components of internal control are present and functioning.
CC4.1.manual_steps: Automated checks verify that continuous evaluation services are enabled: AWS Config continuously evaluates resource compliance, Security Hub aggregates and scores findings. Manual review of evaluation cadence and internal audit processes is also required.
CC4.1.evidence_template: Continuous evaluation infrastructure was verified: AWS Config recorder active with compliance evaluation, Security Hub enabled for aggregated findings. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

CC4.2.title: Evaluates and communicates internal control deficiencies
CC4.2.description: The entity evaluates and communicates internal control deficiencies in a timely manner to those parties responsible for taking corrective action.
CC4.2.manual_steps: Automated checks verify that deficiency detection and alerting infrastructure is active: GuardDuty detects threats, CloudWatch alarms notify on root account usage. Manual review of escalation procedures and management reporting is also required.
CC4.2.evidence_template: Deficiency communication infrastructure was verified: GuardDuty enabled and findings addressed, root account usage alarm active. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

CC5.1.title: Selects and develops control activities
CC5.1.description: The entity selects and develops control activities that contribute to the mitigation of risks to the achievement of objectives to acceptable levels.
CC5.1.manual_steps: Review control activity selection process, risk-control mapping, and evidence that controls address identified risks.
CC5.1.evidence_template: CC5.1 requires manual assessment. Review control activity selection process and risk-control mapping.

CC5.2.title: Develops general control activities over technology
CC5.2.description: The entity selects and develops general control activities over technology to support the achievement of objectives.
CC5.2.evidence_template: Technology controls were verified across IAM (MFA, password policy), encryption (KMS rotation, key policies, RDS, EFS), storage (S3 public access blocks, encryption), compute (EBS encryption), and network (security groups, default SG restrictions). {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

CC5.3.title: Deploys control activities through policies
CC5.3.description: The entity deploys control activities through policies that establish what is expected and in procedures that put policies into action.
CC5.3.manual_steps: Automated checks verify that IAM enforces policy-based controls: no wildcard admin privileges, permissions assigned through groups. Manual review of policy documentation and deployment procedures is also required.
CC5.3.evidence_template: Policy-based control deployment was verified: IAM policies checked for admin privilege restrictions, group-based permission enforcement. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

CC6.1.title: Implements logical access security over protected assets
CC6.1.section: CC6 - Logical and Physical Access Controls
CC6.1.description: The entity implements logical access security software, infrastructure, and architectures over protected information assets to protect them from security events.
CC6.1.evidence_template: Logical access security was verified: MFA enforcement (root and IAM users), password policy strength, KMS key rotation and policies, S3 public access blocks and encryption, EBS/RDS/EFS encryption at rest. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

CC6.2.title: Registers and authorizes users, removes access when no longer needed
CC6.2.section: CC6 - Logical and Physical Access Controls
CC6.2.description: Prior to issuing system credentials and granting system access, the entity registers and authorizes new users. User system credentials are removed when user access is no longer authorized.
CC6.2.evidence_template: User lifecycle management was verified: access key rotation within 90 days, unused credentials disabled after 45 days, single active access key per user, expired SSL/TLS certificates removed. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

CC6.3.title: Authorizes access based on least privilege and segregation of duties
CC6.3.section: CC6 - Logical and Physical Access Controls
CC6.3.description: The entity authorizes, modifies, or removes access to data, software, functions, and other protected information assets based on roles, responsibilities, or system design, applying least privilege and segregation of duties.
CC6.3.evidence_template: Least privilege and segregation of duties were verified: no wildcard admin policies, OIDC trust with sub condition, no root access keys, group-based permissions, CloudShell access restricted, EC2 instances use IAM roles instead of long-lived access keys, ECS Exec restricted. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

CC6.4.title: Restricts physical access to facilities
CC6.4.section: CC6 - Logical and Physical Access Controls
CC6.4.description: The entity restricts physical access to facilities and protected information assets to authorized personnel.
CC6.4.manual_steps: Physical access to AWS data centers is managed by AWS under the shared responsibility model (covered by AWS's own SOC 2 report). Review physical security controls for customer-managed facilities (offices, on-premises equipment).
CC6.4.evidence_template: CC6.4 requires manual assessment. Physical data center security is covered by AWS's SOC 2 report under the shared responsibility model. Review customer facility physical access controls.

CC6.5.title: Discontinues protections after asset disposal
CC6.5.section: CC6 - Logical and Physical Access Controls
CC6.5.description: The entity discontinues logical and physical protections over physical assets only after the ability to read or recover data and software from those assets has been diminished.
CC6.5.manual_steps: Review asset disposal procedures, data sanitization policies, and media destruction records. AWS handles physical media destruction (covered by AWS's SOC 2 report).
CC6.5.evidence_template: CC6.5 requires manual assessment. AWS handles physical media destruction per their SOC 2 report. Review customer data sanitization and asset disposal procedures.

CC6.6.title: Implements boundary protection against external threats
CC6.6.section: CC6 - Logical and Physical Access Controls
CC6.6.description: The entity implements logical access security measures to protect against threats from sources outside its system boundaries.
CC6.6.evidence_template: Boundary protection was verified: security groups restrict admin ports, NACLs block unrestricted access, default SGs restrict all traffic, default VPC not in use, VPC flow logs enabled, no public RDS/AMIs/Lambda URLs, CloudTrail S3 bucket not public, S3 public access blocked. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

CC6.7.title: Protects information during transmission, movement, and removal
CC6.7.section: CC6 - Logical and Physical Access Controls
CC6.7.description: The entity restricts the transmission, movement, and removal of information to authorized internal and external users and processes, and protects it during transmission, movement, or removal.
CC6.7.evidence_template: Data protection in transit and at rest was verified: S3 HTTPS enforcement, S3 MFA Delete, storage encryption (S3 KMS, EBS, RDS, EFS), KMS key rotation, CloudTrail log encryption, SSM SecureString parameters, no secrets in Lambda env vars, Secrets Manager rotation. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

CC6.8.title: Prevents or detects unauthorized or malicious software
CC6.8.section: CC6 - Logical and Physical Access Controls
CC6.8.description: The entity implements controls to prevent or detect and act upon the introduction of unauthorized or malicious software.
CC6.8.manual_steps: Automated checks verify threat detection (GuardDuty), deprecated runtime detection (Lambda), and container privilege restrictions (ECS). Manual review of endpoint protection, application whitelisting, and malware scanning policies may also be required.
CC6.8.evidence_template: Unauthorized software controls were verified: GuardDuty threat detection enabled and findings addressed, Lambda deprecated runtimes detected, ECS containers checked for privileged mode. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

CC7.1.title: Detects and monitors configuration changes and new vulnerabilities
CC7.1.description: The entity uses detection and monitoring procedures to identify changes to configurations that result in the introduction of new vulnerabilities, and susceptibilities to newly discovered vulnerabilities.
CC7.1.evidence_template: Vulnerability detection and change monitoring was verified: Security Hub enabled, AWS Config recorder active with change tracking, IAM Access Analyzer enabled for external access analysis, EC2 instances managed by SSM for patching. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

CC7.2.title: Monitors for anomalies indicative of malicious acts or errors
CC7.2.description: The entity monitors system components and the operation of those components for anomalies that are indicative of malicious acts, natural disasters, and errors affecting the entity's ability to meet its objectives.
CC7.2.evidence_template: Anomaly monitoring was verified: GuardDuty threat detection enabled, VPC flow logs active, ECS task logging, CloudWatch metric filters and alarms for root usage, unauthorized API calls, MFA-less sign-in, IAM/CloudTrail/Config/S3/SG/NACL/gateway/route/VPC/Organizations changes, auth failures, CMK disable/deletion. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

CC7.3.title: Evaluates security events for potential incidents
CC7.3.description: The entity evaluates security events to determine whether they could or have resulted in a failure of the entity to meet its objectives and, if so, takes actions to prevent or address such failures.
CC7.3.manual_steps: Automated checks verify that audit trail infrastructure supports event evaluation: CloudTrail multi-region logging with validation, S3 bucket logging, KMS encryption, object-level logging, S3 access logging, GuardDuty findings addressed. Manual review of event triage and escalation procedures is also required.
CC7.3.evidence_template: Security event evaluation infrastructure was verified: CloudTrail enabled with log validation, S3 bucket access logging, KMS encryption, object-level write/read logging, S3 access logging enabled, GuardDuty findings addressed. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

CC7.4.title: Responds to identified security incidents
CC7.4.description: The entity responds to identified security incidents by executing a defined incident response program to understand, contain, remediate, and communicate security incidents, as appropriate.
CC7.4.manual_steps: Review incident response plan, evidence of tabletop exercises, incident classification criteria, escalation procedures, and records of past incident handling.
CC7.4.evidence_template: CC7.4 requires manual assessment. Review incident response plan, tabletop exercise records, and incident handling procedures.

CC7.5.title: Identifies and implements activities to recover from incidents
CC7.5.description: The entity identifies, develops, and implements activities to recover from identified security incidents.
CC7.5.manual_steps: Review disaster recovery plan, business continuity procedures, recovery time objectives (RTO), recovery point objectives (RPO), and evidence of recovery testing.
CC7.5.evidence_template: CC7.5 requires manual assessment. Review disaster recovery plan, RTO/RPO definitions, and recovery testing records.

CC8.1.title: Authorizes, designs, tests, approves, and implements changes
CC8.1.description: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, configures, documents, tests, approves, and implements changes to infrastructure, data, software, and procedures to meet its objectives.
CC8.1.manual_steps: Automated checks verify change detection: AWS Config tracks configuration changes, CloudWatch alarms detect unauthorized modifications to CloudTrail, Config, security groups, NACLs, gateways, routes, and VPCs. Manual review of change management procedures, approval workflows, and testing requirements is also required.
CC8.1.evidence_template: Change detection infrastructure was verified: AWS Config recorder active, CloudWatch alarms for CloudTrail/Config/security group/NACL/gateway/route table/VPC changes. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

CC9.1.title: Identifies and mitigates risks from business disruptions
CC9.1.description: The entity identifies, selects, and develops risk mitigation activities for risks arising from potential business disruptions.
CC9.1.manual_steps: Automated checks verify availability controls: RDS Multi-AZ, S3 versioning, RDS auto minor upgrade, EC2 termination protection. Manual review of business continuity plan, risk mitigation strategies, and disaster recovery procedures is also required.
CC9.1.evidence_template: Business disruption risk mitigation was verified: RDS Multi-AZ failover, S3 versioning for data protection, RDS auto minor upgrade for patch currency, EC2 termination protection. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

CC9.2.title: Assesses and manages vendor and business partner risks
CC9.2.description: The entity assesses and manages risks associated with vendors and business partners.
CC9.2.manual_steps: Review vendor risk management program, third-party risk assessments, vendor SOC 2 reports (including AWS's SOC 2 report from AWS Artifact), and business partner agreements.
CC9.2.evidence_template: CC9.2 requires manual assessment. Review vendor risk management program and third-party SOC reports (obtain AWS SOC 2 report from AWS Artifact).

A1.1.title: Maintains and monitors processing capacity
A1.1.description: The entity maintains, monitors, and evaluates current processing capacity and use of system components to manage capacity demand and enable additional capacity to meet its objectives.
A1.1.manual_steps: Review CloudWatch dashboards for CPU/memory/storage utilization, Auto Scaling configurations, capacity alarms, and capacity planning documentation.
A1.1.evidence_template: A1.1 requires manual assessment. Review CloudWatch capacity monitoring, Auto Scaling policies, and capacity planning documentation.

A1.2.title: Implements backup, recovery, and redundancy infrastructure
A1.2.description: The entity authorizes, designs, develops or acquires, implements, operates, approves, maintains, and monitors environmental protections, software, data backup processes, and recovery infrastructure to meet its objectives.
A1.2.evidence_template: Backup and recovery infrastructure was verified: RDS Multi-AZ failover, S3 versioning, S3 lifecycle policies, EC2 termination protection. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

A1.3.title: Tests recovery plan procedures
A1.3.description: The entity tests recovery plan procedures supporting system recovery to meet its objectives.
A1.3.manual_steps: Review disaster recovery test plans, test execution records, results documentation, and remediation actions from test findings.
A1.3.evidence_template: A1.3 requires manual assessment. Review disaster recovery test plans, execution records, and findings remediation.

C1.1.title: Identifies and maintains confidential information
C1.1.description: The entity identifies and maintains confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.
C1.1.evidence_template: Confidentiality controls were verified: S3 public access blocked and encryption enabled, RDS/EBS/EFS encryption at rest, KMS key rotation and policies, Secrets Manager rotation. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

C1.2.title: Disposes of confidential information securely
C1.2.description: The entity disposes of confidential information to meet the entity's objectives related to confidentiality.
C1.2.manual_steps: Automated checks verify data lifecycle controls: S3 lifecycle policies for automated expiration, unused secrets detection. Manual review of data disposal procedures and records is also required.
C1.2.evidence_template: Data disposal controls were verified: S3 lifecycle policies for data expiration, unused secrets identified. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}

PI1.1.title: Obtains quality information for processing
PI1.1.description: The entity obtains or generates, uses, and communicates relevant, quality information regarding the objectives related to processing, including definitions of data processed and product and service specifications.
PI1.1.manual_steps: Review data processing documentation, input specifications, data quality requirements, and processing objective definitions.
PI1.1.evidence_template: PI1.1 requires manual assessment. Review data processing documentation and quality requirements.

PI1.2.title: Implements controls over system inputs
PI1.2.description: The entity implements policies and procedures over system inputs, including controls over completeness and accuracy, to result in products, services, and reporting to meet the entity's objectives.
PI1.2.manual_steps: Review application-level input validation controls, data completeness checks, and accuracy verification procedures.
PI1.2.evidence_template: PI1.2 requires manual assessment. Review application-level input validation and data completeness controls.

PI1.3.title: Implements controls over system processing
PI1.3.description: The entity implements policies and procedures over system processing to result in products, services, and reporting to meet the entity's objectives.
PI1.3.manual_steps: Review application-level processing controls, error handling procedures, and transaction integrity mechanisms.
PI1.3.evidence_template: PI1.3 requires manual assessment. Review application-level processing controls and error handling procedures.

PI1.4.title: Implements controls over output delivery
PI1.4.description: The entity implements policies and procedures to make available or deliver output completely, accurately, and timely in accordance with specifications to meet the entity's objectives.
PI1.4.manual_steps: Review output delivery controls, completeness verification, and accuracy checks for system outputs.
PI1.4.evidence_template: PI1.4 requires manual assessment. Review output delivery controls and completeness verification procedures.

PI1.5.title: Implements controls over data storage
PI1.5.description: The entity implements policies and procedures to store inputs, items in processing, and outputs completely, accurately, and timely in accordance with system specifications.
PI1.5.manual_steps: Automated checks verify storage integrity controls: S3 versioning protects against accidental deletion/overwrite, RDS and EBS encryption protect data at rest, KMS key rotation ensures encryption key currency. Manual review of data retention policies and storage procedures is also required.
PI1.5.evidence_template: Data storage integrity controls were verified: S3 versioning enabled, RDS encryption at rest, EBS encryption at rest, KMS key rotation for encryption key currency. {pass_count}/{total_count} check(s) passed. {findings_summary}
